title: Outlier Handling
description: Learn systematic methods for identifying and handling outliers using IQR, z-scores, and Cook's distance, and understand how arbitrary outlier removal biases results.
descriptionAr: تعلم الطرق المنهجية لتحديد والتعامل مع القيم الشاذة باستخدام المدى الربيعي ودرجات z ومسافة كوك.

statisticalConcept.name: Outlier Detection and Handling
statisticalConcept.definition: Outliers are observations that deviate markedly from other members of the sample. Common detection methods include: IQR method (values beyond Q1−1.5×IQR or Q3+1.5×IQR), z-score method (|z|>3), and Cook's distance (Di > 4/n or Di > 1 for regression). Outlier removal without pre-specified criteria is a researcher degree of freedom that can inflate false positive rates.
statisticalConcept.definitionAr: القيم الشاذة هي ملاحظات تنحرف بشكل ملحوظ عن باقي أفراد العينة. تشمل طرق الاكتشاف: طريقة المدى الربيعي، طريقة درجة z، ومسافة كوك.
statisticalConcept.realWorldExample: Anscombe's quartet (1973) demonstrates that identical summary statistics can arise from vastly different datasets, some containing influential outliers. In climate science, removing legitimate extreme temperature readings can mask genuine trends.
statisticalConcept.detectionMethod: IQR fences (1.5×IQR from quartiles), z-scores (|z|>2 or 3), Grubbs' test (single outlier), Cook's distance (regression influence), DFBETAS, and leverage-residual plots. Always pre-specify criteria and report results with and without outliers.

exercises.prompt: A dataset has Q1=25, Q3=75, so IQR=50. Using the standard 1.5×IQR rule, what are the lower and upper fences for outlier detection?
exercises.promptAr: مجموعة بيانات Q1=25، Q3=75، لذا IQR=50. باستخدام قاعدة 1.5×IQR القياسية، ما هي الحدود السفلية والعلوية لاكتشاف القيم الشاذة؟
options: Lower fence = −50, Upper fence = 150
Lower fence = 0, Upper fence = 100
Lower fence = 25, Upper fence = 75
Lower fence = −25, Upper fence = 125
exercises.explanation: Lower fence = Q1 − 1.5×IQR = 25 − 1.5(50) = 25 − 75 = −50. Upper fence = Q3 + 1.5×IQR = 75 + 1.5(50) = 75 + 75 = 150. Any observation below −50 or above 150 is flagged as a potential outlier. Note: Tukey (1977) also defined 'far outliers' at 3×IQR from the quartiles.
exercises.explanationAr: الحد السفلي = 25 − 75 = −50. الحد العلوي = 75 + 75 = 150. أي ملاحظة أقل من −50 أو أعلى من 150 تُعتبر قيمة شاذة محتملة.

exercises.prompt: A regression analysis with 200 observations identifies one data point with Cook's distance Di = 0.85. The common threshold is Di > 4/n. Should this observation be investigated?
exercises.promptAr: تحليل انحدار بـ 200 ملاحظة يحدد نقطة بيانات واحدة بمسافة كوك Di = 0.85. العتبة الشائعة هي Di > 4/n. هل يجب التحقيق في هذه الملاحظة؟
options: No, because Di < 1
Yes: 4/n = 4/200 = 0.02, and 0.85 >> 0.02. This point has extreme influence on the regression and should be investigated
Only if the residual is also large
Cook's distance is not relevant for regression
exercises.explanation: Cook's distance measures the influence of each observation on all fitted values. With n=200, the threshold is 4/200 = 0.02. A Cook's distance of 0.85 is 42.5 times the threshold, indicating this single observation has extraordinary influence on the regression coefficients. Investigation should determine if it represents a data error, a legitimately different population, or a genuine extreme value. Results should be reported with and without this observation.
exercises.explanationAr: مسافة كوك تقيس تأثير كل ملاحظة على جميع القيم المتوقعة. مع ن=200، العتبة هي 0.02. مسافة كوك 0.85 هي 42.5 ضعف العتبة، مما يشير إلى تأثير استثنائي.

exercises.prompt: A researcher runs an experiment and finds p=0.08 (non-significant). They then identify 2 'outliers' using no pre-specified criterion, remove them, and obtain p=0.03 (significant). They report only the final result. What is the methodological problem?
exercises.promptAr: باحث يجري تجربة ويجد p=0.08 (غير معنوي). ثم يحدد 'قيمتين شاذتين' بدون معيار محدد مسبقاً، يزيلهما، ويحصل على p=0.03 (معنوي). يبلغ فقط عن النتيجة النهائية. ما المشكلة المنهجية؟
options: The p=0.03 result is valid because outliers distort data
Outlier removal is always acceptable if justified post-hoc
This is a researcher degree of freedom: post-hoc outlier removal without pre-specified criteria to achieve significance is a form of p-hacking
Two outliers is too few to affect results
exercises.explanation: Post-hoc outlier removal without pre-specified criteria is a well-documented researcher degree of freedom (Simmons et al., 2011). The researcher's motivation was clearly to achieve significance, not to apply a principled cleaning procedure. Best practice: pre-register outlier criteria, report results both with and without outlier exclusion, and use robust statistical methods that are less sensitive to outliers.
exercises.explanationAr: إزالة القيم الشاذة بعد الحقيقة بدون معايير محددة مسبقاً هي درجة حرية باحث موثقة. أفضل الممارسات: تسجيل معايير القيم الشاذة مسبقاً والإبلاغ عن النتائج مع وبدون الاستبعاد.

exercises.prompt: In a dataset of n=30 from a normal distribution, approximately how many observations would you expect to fall beyond |z|>2 by chance alone?
exercises.promptAr: في مجموعة بيانات من ن=30 من توزيع طبيعي، كم ملاحظة تتوقع أن تقع خارج |z|>2 بالصدفة وحدها؟
options: About 10 observations
About 15 observations
Zero — values beyond |z|>2 are always outliers
Approximately 1-2 observations (about 4.6% of 30 ≈ 1.4)
exercises.explanation: In a standard normal distribution, approximately 4.56% of observations fall beyond |z|=2 (2.28% in each tail). With n=30: 30 × 0.0456 ≈ 1.4 observations. This means that flagging all |z|>2 observations as 'outliers' and removing them from a normally distributed sample would inappropriately trim legitimate data. The |z|>3 criterion (0.27% expected) is more conservative and more appropriate for small samples.
exercises.explanationAr: في التوزيع الطبيعي، حوالي 4.56% من الملاحظات تقع خارج |z|=2. مع ن=30: حوالي 1.4 ملاحظة. إزالة جميع |z|>2 سيقص بيانات مشروعة.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is the BEST practice for handling outliers in a pre-registered study?
exercises.promptAr: أي مما يلي هو أفضل ممارسة للتعامل مع القيم الشاذة في دراسة مسجلة مسبقاً؟
options: Pre-specify outlier criteria (e.g., |z|>3 or IQR×1.5), report results with and without exclusions, use robust methods (e.g., trimmed means, bootstrapping) as sensitivity analyses
Remove outliers until the results are significant
Remove all values beyond 1 SD from the mean
Never remove any outliers regardless of the reason
exercises.explanation: Best practice includes: (1) pre-specifying outlier criteria before data collection, (2) distinguishing between data errors and legitimate extreme values, (3) reporting results both with and without outlier exclusions as a sensitivity analysis, (4) using robust statistical methods (trimmed means, Winsorizing, bootstrapped CIs, M-estimators) that are less sensitive to outliers. This approach prevents outlier handling from becoming a covert p-hacking tool.
exercises.explanationAr: أفضل الممارسات: تحديد معايير القيم الشاذة مسبقاً، التمييز بين أخطاء البيانات والقيم المتطرفة المشروعة، الإبلاغ عن النتائج مع وبدون الاستبعادات، واستخدام طرق إحصائية متينة.
